title: Idiot's Array (starship)
content:

{{Quote|She's a corvette and her captain is one of my best.|Talon Karrde|<ref name="Conquest">''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]''</ref>}}
The '''''Idiot's Array''''' was a modified [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] and part of [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Talon Karrde]]'s [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]]. During the [[Invasion of Yavin 4]] of the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]], it was used to ferry the [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]] of the [[Jedi Praxeum]] of [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] where they would be out of danger.

==Description==
The ''Idiot's Array'' was a modified [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] owned by [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] chief and [[Information broker/Legends|information broker]], [[Talon Karrde]].<ref name="Conquest" /><ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|II|77|''Idiot's Array''}}</ref> Its name referred to a [[Idiot's Array/Legends|hand]] in the card game [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]].

==History==
{{Quote|You didn't think I was stupid enough to ''stay'' on a flaming ship, did you? Once I got the lock, we went for the escape pods. Horn ran across us in his X-wing doing a slow spiral toward the gas giant.|Shada D'ukal|<ref name="Conquest" />}}
The ''Idiot's Array'' was one of the ships that accompanied the ''[[Wild Karrde]]'' to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] in [[26 ABY/Legends|26 ABY]] during the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] to rescue the students of the [[Jedi Praxeum]] from the [[Invasion of Yavin 4|attack]] of the [[Peace Brigade]] who wanted to deliver them to the [[Yuuzhan Vong]]. After the children, along with their guardians [[Kam Solusar|Kam]] and [[Tionne Solusar|Tionne]] [[Solusar family|Solusar]], were rescued from the surface of Yavin 4 by Talon Karrde, the ''Idiot's Array'', under the command of [[Shada D'ukal]], ferried them, along with Tionne, to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] where [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] found them a safer place to continue their training.<ref name="Conquest" />

After delivering the [[Youngling/Legends|younglings]] to Coruscant, Shada returned with the Idiot's Array to help Karrde rescue the remaining Jedi trainees on Yavin 4: [[Anakin Solo]], [[Tahiri Veila]], [[Valin Horn]], and [[Sannah]]. The Jedi teamed up with [[Remis Vehn]], who had come to Yavin 4 as a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] for the Peace Brigade, and [[Qorl]], an old [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE]] [[Starfighter/Legends|fighter]] pilot who had been living on Yavin 4 for some time now, and together they were able to repair Vehn's ship and make it off the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]. However, the Yuuzhan Vong attacked them in [[Realspace/Legends|space]] so the ''Idiot's Array'', along with the two other fastest ships of Karrde's fleet the ''[[Sudden Demise]]'' and the ''[[Etherway]]'', escorted the ''Wild Karrde'' to try to help the Jedi. The ''Demise'' was destroyed during the first pass and the ''Idiot's Array'' was also hit a little while later but was still able to keep pace with the ''Wild Karrde'' until it took a second hit, this one to the [[Engine/Legends|engines]]. All of the energy in the ''Idiot's Array'' was then transferred to the [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]] so it could get a lock on the Yuuzhan Vong [[Destroyer/Legends|destroyer]] that was attacking Vehn's ship. Once they had obtained a lock the crew, including Shada D'ukal, vacated the ''Idiot's Array'' in the [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pods]], leaving it on [[Autopilot/Legends|autopilot]]. The ship's [[Reactor/Legends|reactor core]] went critical, blowing up both the ''Idiot's Array'' and the Yuuzhan Vong destroyer, leaving only one destroyer for the ''Wild Karrde'' to deal with.<ref name="Conquest" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Idiot's Array'' first appeared in the [[2001]] novel ''[[The New Jedi Order: Edge of Victory I: Conquest]]'' by [[Greg Keyes]] which was the first book in the [[Edge of Victory Duology]] and also part of the larger [[New Jedi Order]] series. Later, it was given an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]].
